rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope before using them in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared variable types in AGREE.
Avoid placing implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; move valid assignments to implementation scopes.
Do not generate AGREE expressions that reference non-existent or unqualified identifiers from the AADL model.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope.
Maintain type consistency in AGREE expressions by matching declared types with expression results.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes, not in component type annexes.
Remove invalid AGREE clauses that reference unresolved or mismatched identifiers rather than attempting to add missing architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Assignments in AGREE must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid referencing AADL-specific or unqualified types.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing references by introducing new architecture elements.
Do not mix implementation-level and type-level references in the same AGREE contract block.
Do not generate AGREE expressions with mismatched or <error> types.
Do not assume that AADL identifiers are automatically available in AGREE without proper scope or import.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
